title: Intake reported issue, design intent, subject agent, and reproduction evidence

evidence.symptom: Telegram /restart did not appear to stop/restart HASHI9.
evidence.expected_behavior: HASHI9 stops, restarts, health verifies, WatchTower writes restart state/audit/log evidence.
evidence.observed_behavior: HASHI9 stayed online; no WatchTower restart state/audit/stdout evidence.

baseline_facts: HASHI9 running process predated /restart implementation commit, so first reported Telegram test could not have loaded the new command.
WatchTower restart routes were live and authenticated.
Initial controller restart smoke reached WatchTower but failed verify after stop/start.

facts: hashiko confirmed running HASHI9 main.py started before /restart code was committed.
hashiko confirmed WatchTower /control/hashi/restart and /control/hashi/restarts/{restart_id} routes existed.
hashiko found no live /restart handler/log evidence for the failed user test.
root_cause_candidates: Old running HASHI9 process had not loaded /restart.
WatchTower service-context launcher was not reliably starting HASHI9 healthy.

title: Reviewer pre-implementation criteria and plan review

evidence.plan_verdict: Proceed with bounded WatchTower launcher fix and real smoke; keep HASHI core unchanged.

evidence.core_boundary_decision: Keep HASHI core slim; WatchTower owns supervised cold start and uses fixed direct python launcher only.
evidence.rollback_path: Revert WatchTower commit and restart HashiWatchtower service.

behavior_changed: Windows WatchTower start uses fixed direct python main.py launcher when available.
Windows start environment adds user tool paths for claude/npm and sets BRIDGE_HOME.
WatchTower infers C:\Users\thene\hashi9-home as controlled bridge home when present.
Restart verify window increased to 45 seconds.

evidence.diff_review: Controller reviewed bounded diff locally after smoke-driven fixes.
evidence.blocker_status: No blocker remains after final smoke.

title: Real-world subject-agent smoke and log audit

evidence.audit: remote_rescue_audit.jsonl recorded outcome=restarted with restart_id restart-fc7fbc153e484337b789a4386be9f075

fixes: Replaced unreliable service-context PowerShell Start-Process startup with fixed direct python launcher.
Corrected bridge-home to C:\Users\thene\hashi9-home so API gateway state survives cold restart.
Augmented Windows PATH for service-context CLI availability.
retests: restart-352de460955c41c19040220658fc0835 completed but exposed wrong bridge-home/API disabled.
restart-fc7fbc153e484337b789a4386be9f075 completed with API enabled and default model preserved.

evidence.root_cause: The initial live test used an old HASHI9 process that had not loaded /restart; subsequent WatchTower service-context start attempts failed because launcher environment/bridge-home were wrong.
final_checks: WatchTower route accepted restart and wrote durable restart state.
Workbench health returned ok with hashiko online.
API gateway persisted state remained enabled with default model claude-haiku-4-5.
